commit_message:
test(adw): scaffold timeout/budget/resume failure-drill runbook

Turn the terse "Failure-mode drills" bullets in LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md into a
standalone, copy-paste live `claude` runbook and close the one uncovered
fail-fast branch with a deterministic test.

Docs:
- Add adw_sdlc/docs/FAILURE-DRILLS.md: three drills (timeout fast-fail,
  budget fast-fail, kill-then--resume) each with an exact command and the
  expected signal/outcome. Expected-signal text quotes the verbatim AdwError
  strings (run-phase.ts:136/139/144) and anchors every claim to source lines
  so a future editor can re-verify. The nudge-retry case is documented as
  opportunistic-only with NO recipe to force an output-contract violation.
- LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md: add full-runbook pointer from "Failure-mode drills",
  mark issue #4 as run in the Status block, and retire the "Land issue #4
  first" instruction from the nudge-retry bullet.
- README.md: add a row to the "Documentation map" table for FAILURE-DRILLS.md,
  and add --timeout / --max-budget-usd to the frequently-used flags table with
  a one-line note on their fail-fast behaviour (no nudge retry).

Tests (deterministic, mocked, no network/spend):
- run-phase.test.ts: add the missing `signal:'cancelled'` fail-fast-no-nudge
  case (timeout/budget were already covered); asserts one request, no
  transcript-2.log, error matches /cancelled/.
- orchestrator.test.ts: pin that `--timeout` / `--max-budget-usd` thread from
  RunOptions into every runAgentPhase call (the Drill 1 / Drill 2 wiring).

No src/ behavior changed; docs + tests only. `npm run verify` green
(typecheck, lint:env, pack:check, 505 tests, build).

closes #4
pr_body:
## What

Scaffolds the ADW failure-mode drill procedure for issue #4 (part of the MVP
live-run batch). Primarily a documentation artifact, plus deterministic tests
that close a real coverage gap and pin the wiring the drills rely on.

## Changes

**Docs**
- **New `adw_sdlc/docs/FAILURE-DRILLS.md`** — a live `claude` runbook with three
  drills, each a copy-paste command + expected outcome:
  1. **Timeout fast-fail** (`--timeout 1`) → `signal:'timeout'`, no nudge.
  2. **Budget fast-fail** (`--max-budget-usd 0.01`) → `signal:'budget'`, no nudge.
  3. **Kill, then `--resume`** → resume prints `skipping <phase> (already completed)`.
  Expected-signal text quotes the **verbatim** `AdwError` strings
  (`run-phase.ts:136/139/144`) and the parse-before-signal nuance
  (`run-phase.ts:126–128`), and anchors each claim to source lines.
- The **nudge-retry** path is documented as *opportunistic only* — no recipe to
  force a JSON-contract violation (per issue Notes / `LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md`).
- **`LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md`**: full-runbook pointer added to the "Failure-mode drills"
  section; Status block updated to note issue #4 as run; "Land issue #4 first"
  instruction retired now that the scaffold is available.
- **`README.md`**: row added to the "Documentation map" table for `FAILURE-DRILLS.md`;
  `--timeout` and `--max-budget-usd` added to the frequently-used flags table with
  a one-line note on their fail-fast behaviour (`signal:'timeout'` / `signal:'budget'`, no nudge retry).

**Tests** (deterministic, mocked, no network/spend)
- `test/run-phase.test.ts`: adds the missing **`signal:'cancelled'`**
  fail-fast-no-nudge case (timeout/budget were already covered at `:191`/`:202`).
  Asserts one runner request, no `transcript-2.log`, error matches `/cancelled/`.
- `test/orchestrator.test.ts`: two tests pinning that **`--timeout` /
  `--max-budget-usd` thread from `RunOptions` into every `runAgentPhase` call**
  (the Drill 1 / Drill 2 plumbing).

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] Each of the three drills has a copy-paste command and expected outcome.
- [x] Any added test is deterministic (mocked runner; no network/spend).
- [x] `npm run verify` stays green.

Reviewer done-checks: no force-misbehave recipe; expected-signal text matches the
verbatim error strings; cross-links added; **no `src/` changed** (docs + tests only).

## Verification

`npm run verify` from `adw_sdlc/` — exit 0
(typecheck → lint:env → pack:check → **505 tests, 38 files** → build → rm dist).

## Review notes (non-blocking)

- `orchestrator.test.ts` is extended in addition to the spec-named
  `run-phase.test.ts`. Both additions are test-only, deterministic, and directly
  strengthen the drills (they prove the CLI flags actually reach the invoker).
- The runbook cites exact source line numbers (brittle by nature). Mitigated as
  the spec intends: error messages are **quoted, not paraphrased**, and the new
  `cancelled` test pins that message in CI.
- Running the live drills (real spend) remains a separate, human-driven step;
  this change only scaffolds the procedure and does not flip any ledger rows.

closes #4

review_findings.description: Tests added to orchestrator.test.ts (2 cases pinning that --timeout/--max-budget-usd thread into every runAgentPhase call) go beyond the spec's named test scope (run-phase.test.ts cancelled case only). Justified: deterministic, mocked, no spend, and they close a real gap making the documented Drill 1/Drill 2 commands end-to-end meaningful. Disclosed in PR body.

FAILURE-DRILLS.md hardcodes exact source line numbers (e.g. run-phase.ts:136/139/144, run-phase.test.ts:191/202, orchestrator.ts:1114-1116) that will silently drift as code moves. Mitigated per the spec's intent — error messages are quoted verbatim (not paraphrased) and the new cancelled test pins those strings in CI — but the citations themselves remain a maintenance footgun.
